content.problemStatement:
Read problem statements in RussianChef is having a candlelit dinner with his girlfriend at his place. Currently, it is time 000, Chef just managed to light a candle, but he is out of matchsticks.

Since Chef has an infinite supply of (unlit) candles, he can use the following strategy: each second (starting at time 000), he takes one lit candle and lights up XXX other candles using it. This process takes 111 unit of time to complete, i.e. if Chef starts lighting up XXX candles at a time t1t_1t1​, he is able to finish at the time t1+1t_1 + 1t1​+1.

However, since candle wax is limited, a candle can only burn for YYY units of time and then it is extinguished.

Chef's girlfriend arrives at a time AAA — specifically, after all the candles that were supposed to get extinguished at time AAA have been extinguished and after Chef lights up all the candles that he was lighting up since time A−1A-1A−1. Now Chef wants to know the maximum number of candles which can be lit at the time AAA to impress his girlfriend.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers AAA, YYY and XXX.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of lit candles Chef can impress his girlfriend with.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: At the time 000, there is one lit candle, using which we light 111 more candle. At the time 111, the starting candle gets extinguished and the candle which we started lighting up at the time 000 is lit, so we have only 111 candle lit up when Chef's girlfriend arrives.
Example case 2: At the time 000, there is one lit candle, using which we light 111 more candle. At the time 111, the candle which we started lighting up at the time 000 and the starting candle are both lit.
Example case 3: At the time 000, there is one lit candle, using which we light 111 more candle. At the time 111, the candle which we started lighting up at time 000 is lit, and we start lighting up one more candle. At the time 222, the starting candle gets extinguished and the candle which we started lighting up at the time 111 is lit. In total, we have 222 lit candles when Chef's girlfriend arrives.
